name: Engen

quantity_rules.description: Fuel lines show: [Fuel type]  P [pump#]  [price_per_litre]  R  [total_amount]. The quantity (litres) is NOT printed — calculate it as total_price / unit_price.
examples: Diesel 50ppm  P 6  17.390  R  600.00  →  name='Diesel 50ppm', unit_type='litre', unit_price=17.390, total_price=600.00, quantity=round(600.00/17.390, 2)=34.50
Unleaded 93  P 3  22.150  R  443.00  →  name='Unleaded 93', unit_type='litre', unit_price=22.150, total_price=443.00, quantity=round(443.00/22.150, 2)=20.00

product_name_rules.description: Use the fuel grade as the item name. Ignore pump number (P n) — it is not part of the name.

store_name_rules.description: Normalise store name to 'Engen' followed by the station name if visible.

prompt_notes: Fuel quantity (litres) is never printed on the receipt. Always calculate: quantity = round(total_price / unit_price, 2).
Set unit_type='litre' and per_kg_price=unit_price for all fuel items so the price-per-litre is stored.
Set category='fuel' for all fuel items.
VAT on fuel in South Africa is typically included in the pump price — set vat_applicable=true.
The P number (e.g. 'P 6') is the pump number — do not include it in the item name.
There is usually only one fuel line item per receipt.
